message:
fix(model): accept case-insensitive and unknown IssueType values via custom Deserialize

Extends the permissive-Deserialize pattern introduced for Status in 397a59e
to the IssueType enum. Replaces the derived serde(rename_all = "snake_case")
Deserialize with a hand-rolled impl that lowercases the incoming string,
maps known variants (task, bug, feature, epic, chore, docs, question) to
their enum values, and stashes any unrecognized string into
IssueType::Custom(value).

Why:
- The merge_issue fuzz harness and real-world user JSONL both emit issue_type
  strings that don't match the canonical snake_case set — e.g. "Bug",
  "CHORE", or project-specific labels like "incident" or "spike". The
  derived Deserialize rejected these, causing import failures that the
  fuzzer treats as crashes.
- The Custom(String) variant already exists for exactly this purpose;
  previously it was only reachable via FromStr. Wiring the same permissive
  path into serde's deserialization lets JSONL round-trip preserve
  unexpected types instead of dropping issues.

Behavior matches the Status counterpart:
- Canonical snake_case variants: matched directly
- Case-insensitive variants ("BUG", "Task", etc): matched after to_lowercase
- Anything else: stored verbatim in IssueType::Custom(value)

Serialization remains unchanged (derived Serialize with rename_all), so
output still emits canonical snake_case for the known set.
